definition: Backward-looking standard deviation of underlying returns
source_or_model: Computed from the underlying price series
implementation_note: Every analyst using the same window and returns definition gets the same number

definition: Volatility that actually occurs over the holding period
source_or_model: Computed after the fact from realized returns
implementation_note: Use it to score whether implied volatility was rich or cheap

definition: Forward-looking volatility backed out from option market price
source_or_model: Invert an option pricing model such as BSM
implementation_note: It is contract-specific, not one number for the underlying

definition: IV changes by strike for the same expiry
implementation_note: OTM puts are often rich because crash insurance is demanded

definition: IV changes by time to expiry for the same strike or moneyness
implementation_note: Do not compare contracts without matching strike/moneyness and expiry

definition: Three-dimensional map of strike, expiry, and IV
implementation_note: The surface tells which contract is relatively cheap or rich
